title: lead structural test engineer - UAV systems
description:
advanced aerospace solutions is looking for a lead structural test engineer (UAV systems) in Monterey, CA.
This position is onsite in Monterey, CA, and remote work is not available.
job overview:
As a lead structural test engineer, you will oversee the testing processes for unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) components. In our state-of-the-art test facility, you will develop test strategies, prepare comprehensive test plans, and lead a team to execute structural tests. The insights gained from these tests will contribute significantly to the development of reliable UAV designs that meet rigorous industry standards.
responsibilities:
Design and implement test setups to evaluate the structural integrity of UAV components, focusing on lightweight materials. Collaborate with design engineers to understand load scenarios and operational requirements. Draft detailed test plans, procedures, and data acquisition strategies to ensure accuracy and repeatability of test results. Analyze test data and compile reports for engineering teams to refine design approaches, ensuring optimal performance and safety.
required:
Proven experience in structural testing within an engineering lab environment. Hands-on experience with composite materials and testing methodologies. Strong background in structural engineering principles, particularly as they relate to aerospace applications. Familiarity with data acquisition systems, including sensors like strain gauges and accelerometers. Proficient in CAD software for mechanical design and drafting. Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills, with strong communication abilities. B.S. in Engineering with 10+ years of relevant experience or M.S. with 8+ years.
desired:
5+ years of experience in UAV testing and certification processes. Background in compliance with FAA regulations for UAV systems. Familiarity with advanced testing technologies, including digital image correlation and acoustic emission testing. Practical skills in machine shop operations and prototyping techniques. Experience with software like SolidWorks for design and analysis.
For consideration, please submit your resume to hr@advancedaerospacesolutions.com.
advanced aerospace solutions, inc. is a leading provider of engineering staffing and consulting services, specializing in aerospace and defense industries. We take pride in connecting talented professionals with top-tier employers across the nation. Our dedicated recruiting team ensures personalized assistance in finding the right job opportunities that align with your career goals.
We offer competitive benefits, including health insurance, 401(k) plans, and performance bonuses for eligible positions. Advanced aerospace solutions, inc. is an equal opportunity employer.
skills_desc: structural testing, UAV systems, composite materials

description:

the greater dallas tollway authority has been enhancing travel for dallas residents for over 20 years. we maintain a network of efficient and secure roadways, catering to millions of commuters annually while contributing to the region's economic vitality. as pioneers in tolling innovation, we aim to uphold our reputation for cutting-edge transportation solutions.

title: postdoctoral research fellow - immunotherapy
description:
job description

position summary

a postdoctoral research fellow position is available in dr. john smith’s lab within the department of biomedical sciences, focusing on immunotherapy approaches for cancer treatment. our research emphasizes the modulation of immune responses to enhance the efficacy of therapeutic interventions. we employ an array of methodologies such as in vitro assays, gene editing, and advanced animal models to explore the mechanisms of immune activation and tumor interaction.

we are looking for a postdoctoral researcher dedicated to studying immune checkpoint inhibitors and their role in cancer therapy. the successful candidate will have the chance to collaborate on projects related to immune cell profiling and therapeutic efficacy in preclinical models. candidates will be encouraged to pursue their research interests within the fields of immunology, oncology, and gene therapy.

primary duties and responsibilities

under the guidance of a faculty mentor, the candidate will:

- lead independent research projects aimed at producing high-impact publications.
- gain expertise in flow cytometry, immune cell manipulation, and various in vivo experimental techniques.
- develop critical skills including scientific writing, public speaking, and analytical thinking.
- present findings at conferences and departmental meetings.
- assist in grant writing and funding applications.
- design experiments, collect and analyze data, and maintain accurate lab records.

required qualifications

applicants must hold a phd or equivalent in a relevant field.

preferred qualifications

the ideal candidate should have experience in cancer immunology, flow cytometry, and mouse models. candidates with diverse research backgrounds who can apply their skills to our focus areas are also welcome. strong communication skills, both verbal and written, are essential.

working conditions

this position operates in a laboratory setting with possible exposure to biological and chemical materials. the individual must be able to adhere to safety protocols and manage research animals as needed.

salary range

salary will be based on experience levels.

applicant special instructions

interested candidates should submit 1) a cover letter detailing their research background and career aspirations, 2) a cv, and 3) contact information for 2-3 references to dr. john smith (john.smith@missouri.edu).

questions

for additional information regarding the application process, please consult our applicant faq section.

accommodation

if you require assistance with the application process, please contact us at candidatequestions@missouri.edu or call our accommodation line at 573-882-1234.

pre-employment screening

all candidates offered a position must undergo pre-employment screening, which may include background checks, drug tests, and other assessments based on the role. all offers are contingent upon successful completion of this process.

benefits statement

the university of missouri is committed to equal employment opportunities and encourages applications from individuals of diverse backgrounds. we adhere to policies that prohibit discrimination on any basis, including race, gender, and disability.

title: WMO Specialist (Allround)
description:
Wat ga je doen?

Als WMO Specialist ben je verantwoordelijk voor het analyseren en beoordelen van hulpvragen binnen de WMO. Je stelt samen met betrokkenen een passend ondersteuningsvoorstel op en voert administratieve taken uit. Je hebt ervaring met het voeren van gesprekken met inwoners en kunt hun hulpvragen helder krijgen. Je werkt vanuit de eigen kracht van de hulpvrager en stimuleert het gebruik van beschikbare voorzieningen. Kostenbewustzijn en samenwerking met andere zorgaanbieders zijn ook belangrijke aspecten van je functie. Je zorgt voor een zorgvuldige overdracht en houdt toezicht op de uitvoering van zorg- en ondersteuningsplannen.

Wie zijn wij?

De gemeente Malden is gelegen in een aantrekkelijke omgeving met diverse stedelijke faciliteiten. Wij hechten veel waarde aan leefbaarheid, participatie en duurzaamheid. We investeren in een sterk netwerk met diverse partners en vervullen een verbindende rol tussen inwoners, organisaties en overheden. Professionele ontwikkeling en een goede werk-privébalans zijn voor ons van groot belang.

Wat bieden wij jou?

Een parttime functie van 24 uur per week in een dynamische organisatie; een salaris dat kan oplopen tot €4850,- bruto per maand op basis van een 36-urige werkweek; een tijdelijk contract met uitzicht op een vast dienstverband; pensioenopbouw bij ABP, een bijdrage voor ziektekosten en een fitnessabonnement; een individueel keuzebudget van 17,05% van je bruto maandsalaris, dat je kunt besteden aan persoonlijke wensen; een laptop, mobiele telefoon en budget voor thuiswerken; een reiskostenvergoeding voor woon-werkverkeer.

Wie zoeken wij?

Wij zoeken een empathische en sociale WMO Specialist die ervaring heeft met het indiceren van WMO-voorzieningen en beschikt over een relevante HBO-opleiding. Je bent energiek, flexibel en creatief en hebt een sterke interesse in de inwoners met hulpvragen. Je kunt goed communiceren en werkt samen met collega's en partners om de zelfredzaamheid van de inwoners te vergroten.

Waar kom je te werken?

Bij de gemeente Malden werken we hybride, wat zorgt voor flexibiliteit en een betere balans tussen werk en privé. Je werkt deels op kantoor en deels vanuit huis. Parkeren is gratis en dichtbij.

Hoe solliciteer je?

Ben je geïnteresseerd? Solliciteer dan snel, maar uiterlijk op 15 mei via onze website.

Voor vragen kun je contact opnemen met Lisa van Dijk, Hoofd Sociale Zaken, op 06-98765432.

Goed om te weten

De eerste gespreksronde is gepland op 18 mei. Een geldige VOG is vereist voor indiensttreding. Wij willen een inclusieve werkomgeving creëren waar iedereen zich welkom voelt. Herken je jezelf in het profiel en ben je enthousiast? Solliciteer dan snel!

description:
coca-cola $28.50 / hour

view our warehouse associate (loader) job preview video here.

$28.50 per hour

full-time / evening shift

work schedule: 4 days a week, 10 hours a day.

saturdays are mandatory.

shift starts at 3:00pm mon-fri; 12:00pm on saturday.

this physically demanding role involves assembling pallets of products for bulk and route deliveries. attention to detail is crucial for order accuracy in loading outgoing trucks and unloading incoming shipments. utilize an electronic order system or order sheets to construct loads using a forklift and/or electric pallet jack. responsible for ensuring accurate loading and adhering to shipping/receiving documentation. maintain a clean and safe work environment. flexibility to work in various weather conditions and hours; shift duration may vary based on workload.

bulk truck loading includes selecting specific packages and product types from the warehouse and stacking them onto a pallet. maintaining pallet stability through proper stacking techniques and shrink-wrapping is essential. typically, pallets are then loaded onto a delivery truck. responsible for unloading returned items and pallets.

route truck loading requires selecting designated packages from the warehouse and loading them directly into the truck based on salesperson orders. also responsible for unloading returned products.

the job requires lifting a high volume of product cases daily, ranging from 20-50 pounds, and involves constant standing, walking, bending, and twisting as items are moved.

primary responsibilities:

driving power pallet jack or forklift to assembly area and constructing pallet/load according to order sheet using voice pick audio equipment (if applicable).
marking load tickets for out-of-stock items.
wrapping pallets upon completion.
submitting paperwork with orders for verification before loading and ensuring truck checks are completed.
organizing pallets and loading them onto trucks when ready.
maintaining cleanliness in the workspace.
regular, reliable attendance is critical.

basic requirements:

must be at least 18 years old.

preferred experience:

previous warehouse experience (e.g., order selecting, product staging, using powered pallet jacks/forklifts).
operating a forklift (e.g., basic driving, maneuvering in tight spaces, safely lifting and lowering loads).
experience with racking systems and pallet handling.
ability to work collaboratively in a team environment.

title: senior counselor, college & career (kipp woodson)
description:
company description

kipp texas public schools is committed to providing high-quality education through a network of 59 public charter schools serving nearly 34,000 students across major cities in Texas. Our mission is to establish vibrant, academically rigorous schools that equip students with the skills and confidence necessary for their future endeavors—be it college, careers, or other paths—ensuring they can lead meaningful lives and contribute to a fairer society.

Founded in Houston in 1994 and rebranded as kipp texas in 2018, we prioritize diversity, equity, inclusion, and antiracism in all our efforts. We are eager to welcome talented, collaborative, and passionate individuals who believe in the potential of every child. Join us in our mission to champion equity, pursue excellence, and foster a supportive community.

KIPP Texas is part of a broader network of 255 college-prep public charter schools across 20 states and Washington D.C. Our students achieve a college completion rate of 36 percent, aligning with national averages and outperforming students from low-income backgrounds.

Explore our job openings at https://kipptexas.org/join-our-team/.

job description

the opportunity

KIPP Texas - San Antonio is looking for a seasoned college access expert to become a part of the KIPP Forward counseling team at one of our high school campuses as a senior counselor. In this role, you will foster relationships with school leadership, local organizations, and institutions of higher education to enhance resources available to students. The overarching aim of the KIPP Forward college counseling team is to empower all graduates with the tools they need for success in their academic, professional, and personal lives.

This position is perfect for a driven, collaborative, and results-oriented leader dedicated to addressing educational disparities in Texas.

key responsibilities

college and career access expertise

KIPP Forward college counseling relies on data and strong connections with students and their families. Guide and mentor all students, including those facing unique challenges, through the financial aid process. Facilitate workshops that introduce families to college resources and career exploration, ensuring a supportive environment for all. Maintain knowledge of college readiness assessments and their implications for access.

collaboration

Work collaboratively with KIPP Forward regional staff, ensuring effective communication with all key stakeholders. Support KIPP seniors through lesson planning, execution, and classroom management while maintaining partnerships with external organizations to enhance student opportunities. Participate in regular check-ins to align on goals and progress.

data management

Communicate consistently about student progress with stakeholders, utilizing various platforms to track and manage data. Monitor and report on KIPP Forward's post-secondary access goals, ensuring all necessary milestones are achieved, including college applications and financial aid submissions.

classroom responsibilities

Design and implement meaningful lesson plans that support KIPP seniors through the college application process. Participate in team leadership activities and contribute to special programming, such as college visits and financial aid workshops. Foster an inclusive classroom culture while meeting all required deadlines set by the school and KIPP Forward team.

physical requirements:

The role requires significant time spent at a desk using a computer and attending training events. Reasonable accommodations can be made for individuals with disabilities.

qualifications

Bachelor’s degree required, with 3+ years of experience in college access or admissions advising preferred. Strong data-driven decision-making skills and experience in public education or non-profit sectors are advantageous. Proficiency in technology and productivity tools is essential.

additional information

compensation and benefits

Salary is commensurate with experience, educational background, and expertise.

As a full-time employee, you will have access to a comprehensive benefits package, including medical, dental, and vision insurance, life insurance, and more, with options starting as low as $25/month.

KIPP Texas is an equal opportunity employer, committed to hiring without regard to race, religion, gender, sexual orientation, or disability.

at liberty mutual, we are committed to helping people live safer, more secure lives. our mission is to support our employees in their professional growth while serving our customers with integrity and dedication. we are a diverse team working together to innovate and enhance our services.

you have the opportunity to join a dynamic team that values collaboration and creativity.

the opportunity

this position is open for remote work within the continental united states with occasional travel required.

relocation assistance is not available for this role.

as part of the telematics analytics team, you will leverage data-driven insights to optimize our auto insurance offerings. your work will directly influence product development and customer engagement strategies.

you will analyze complex datasets to derive actionable insights, develop predictive models, and collaborate with cross-functional teams to implement solutions that drive business outcomes. you will also work closely with software engineers to integrate analytics into production systems and improve data accessibility across the organization.

what you’ll do

you will collect, analyze, and interpret data to support business decisions and strategy. lead projects utilizing predictive modeling and machine learning techniques to enhance operational efficiency. guide the team in selecting appropriate analytical methods considering data constraints and business objectives. develop documentation for model validation and ensure compliance with regulatory standards. collaborate with stakeholders to identify analytics needs and propose solutions that create value. maintain a library of reusable analytical tools and models. assist in translating business queries into analytical tasks and communicate results to a diverse audience. manage project timelines and risks, proactively addressing potential obstacles. establish best practices for data handling and analytics to ensure quality and consistency across projects. stay updated on the latest advancements in data science and analytics methodologies and share knowledge with team members. serve as a mentor to junior analysts, fostering their professional development.

what you have

bachelor’s degree in a quantitative discipline such as statistics, mathematics, or computer science; equivalent experience may be considered in lieu of a degree.5+ years of experience in data analysis or predictive modeling, with a strong background in statistical methods. proficiency in programming languages like python or r for statistical analysis and model development. solid experience with data manipulation and querying using sql or similar languages. demonstrated ability to work with various data formats including json, xml, and flat files. strong problem-solving skills and the ability to communicate complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders. project management experience with a focus on delivering results on time and within scope. familiarity with machine learning algorithms and data visualization tools is a plus.

what sets you apart

experience with telematics data or internet of things (iot) applications. familiarity with cloud platforms like aws or azure is advantageous.

what we offer

compensation: liberty mutual provides a competitive salary based on market data and individual experience. the salary range for this position is: $120,000 - $220,000.

employees may also receive performance-based bonuses and other incentives.

benefits: we offer comprehensive benefits including health, dental, and vision coverage, retirement savings plans, paid time off, and professional development opportunities. our focus is on promoting a balanced lifestyle for our employees.

applications for this position are accepted continuously and will remain open until filled. interested candidates are encouraged to apply promptly.

liberty mutual is an equal opportunity employer. we welcome applications from all qualified individuals without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or status as a protected veteran.

title: senior product design manager
description:
at secure pay solutions, we lead the charge in transforming digital payment systems. with advanced technologies and a strong commitment to security, we empower businesses and consumers to engage in safe transactions. our innovative platform is designed to meet the evolving needs of our clients, helping to streamline their financial operations.

the primary responsibility of this role is to oversee the design process for our suite of products, ensuring user needs are met and exceeded. you will collaborate closely with product managers, developers, and stakeholders to create impactful user experiences that drive engagement and satisfaction. the senior product design manager will mentor junior designers, fostering a creative and collaborative environment that prioritizes user-centered design principles. a strong understanding of design systems, usability testing, and visual communication is essential.

preferred qualifications include experience with agile development methodologies and familiarity with accessibility standards.

working conditions are primarily in an office environment with some flexibility for remote work. the role may involve sitting for extended periods and using a computer for design tasks.

salary for this position in

san francisco, ca ranges from $150,000 to $165,000 per year, with opportunities for bonuses and comprehensive benefits packages that support employee health and well-being, including healthcare, retirement plans, and generous paid time off.

secure pay solutions is an equal opportunity employer, committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace. we welcome candidates from all backgrounds and strive to create an environment where everyone can thrive.

qualificationsinclude a minimum of 3 years of experience in environmental policy or advocacy, with a strong emphasis on indiana legislation. exceptional communication and interpersonal skills are essential, along with a demonstrated ability to lead initiatives and build coalitions. a passion for environmental sustainability and a commitment to community engagement are crucial for success in this role.

full-time position with comprehensive benefits including health, dental, and retirement plans. generous paid time off policy and flexible work hours.
